title: Man charged in Lowell hit-and-run that killed woman, 29
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Guilherme Candido, 23, charged with leaving the scene after fatally hitting a pedestrian in Lowell, turned himself in.
The victim, a 29-year-old woman, died from injuries after being hit on Pawtucket Boulevard; Candido identified via surveillance.
Candido, held on $7,500 bond, must wear GPS and surrender passport if released; ICE to detain post-trial.

title: Mass. detective, in kayak, allegedly shot hole in canoe with his brother on it
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Attleboro Detective James Miller, 52, allegedly shot a hole in a canoe carrying his brother and friend, police reported.
Miller faces charges including felony assault, discharging a firearm near a building, and carrying a firearm while intoxicated.
Miller's police license was suspended after his arrest, and he is currently under psychiatric evaluation at Sturdy Memorial Hospital.

title: 5 takeaways as Payton Tolle and Chris Sale battle before Braves pull away for 10-2 victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox fall to the Atlanta Braves 10-2, marking their fifth loss in six games.
Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle struggled with consistency, allowing two earned runs over 4 2/3 innings.
Boston is now 9-19 at home, having not won a series at Fenway Park since early April.

title: Drake Maye reflects on Super Bowl loss, rewatching game film vs. Seattle
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye reflects on the New England Patriots' 29-13 Super Bowl LX loss to the Seattle Seahawks.
Maye reviews game film to learn from mistakes, noting his two interceptions in the fourth quarter.
The Patriots will face the Seahawks again in their 2026 season opener at Seattle's Lumen Field.

title: Former Bruins enforcer, radio host Lyndon Byers diagnosed with CTE after his death
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Lyndon Byers, former Boston Bruins player, was diagnosed with Stage 3 CTE after his death at 61.
Byers' family shared results from Boston University's CTE Center to raise awareness about the disease's impact.
Byers, known for his physical play, experienced severe depression and memory loss after his NHL career.

title: Drake Maye gives a vote of confidence for Will Campbell after playoff struggles
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Will Campbell faced challenges in the 2025 playoffs, culminating in a poor performance against Seattle in Super Bowl LX.
Drake Maye supported Campbell, citing his own rookie struggles and Campbell's positive attitude towards improvement.
Patriots added Alijah Vera-Tucker to support the offensive line, while drafting Caleb Lomu as a potential future tackle alternative.

title: 2 women attempt to swim from Marblehead to Scituate to qualify for Ireland marathon swims
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Abigail Fairman and Sarah Roberts attempted a 18-mile swim from Marblehead to Scituate to qualify for Irish marathon swims.
Despite choppy waters forcing them to stop short, both swimmers met the qualifications needed for future Irish races.
Fairman and Roberts encountered jellyfish and shifting winds, ultimately turning back before completing their planned route.

title: Preservation efforts ramp up as Swansea Friends Meeting House is named one of nation’s most endangered historic sites
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Swansea Friends Meeting House was listed as one of the 11 most endangered historic sites by the National Trust.
Friends of Somerset Historic Preservation received its largest grant yet, $184,300 from the Mass Cultural Council, for restoration.
The preservation group's plan is to establish a community cultural center in the restored Meeting House.

title: Cape Cod’s Sundae School to offer 76-cent ice cream scoops
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sundae School Homemade Ice Cream will celebrate its 50th anniversary with 76-cent cones or cups on June 14.
The deal honors the shop's 1976 opening and is available from 2-4 p.m. at Harwich Port and Dennisport locations.
An anniversary celebration will also occur at the Harwich Port shop, reflecting the shop's role in Cape Cod tradition.

title: July 4th fireworks cancellation sparks intense pushback in Rutland
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rutland officials canceled July 4th fireworks, parade, and concert due to staffing shortages after a tax override rejection.
Residents are upset over the cancellation and the town's refusal to refund donations meant for the celebrations.
The Massachusetts Fiscal Alliance called for an investigation into the town’s handling of celebration funds.
